description: Collapse a bare-statement wrapper: ``expression_statement`` whose only child is an Operator (or a leftover ``call`` Node that didn't make it to Operator promotion). Without this rule, statements like ``clf.fit(X, y)`` and ``print(score)`` leave two stacked nodes — an expression_statement pointing at the Operator. Then chain_method_shortcuts (60) bumps the expression_statement → Operator edge's position and creates a spurious ``expression_statement → fit (pos="X")`` edge that shadows the real arg wiring. Mirrors python's expression_statement collapse.
